query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "arcane: do role-playing language agents stay in character at the right" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on improving LLM agent performance through offline reinforcement learning and action rescoring, which is a distinct mechanism from the user's question regarding "staying in character" (role consistency) and linguistic analysis. While both involve language agents, the candidate paper does not measure the specific construct of character adherence, nor does it provide a baseline or methodology for the linguistic evaluation of role-playing consistency requested by the user.
